tool_args.label: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
tool_args.raw_text: This performance metrics document was prepared for the December 2022 meeting of the Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees.

payload.title: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.description: Key performance metrics document prepared for the December 2022 meeting of the Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees.

evidence_refs.source_quote: December 2022
This performance metrics document was prepared for the December 2022 meeting of the Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees.
December 19, 2022

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Long Island Rail Road
tool_args.raw_text: Long Island Rail Road is comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

payload.description: Long Island Rail Road is comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Long Island Rail Road is comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

tool_args.label: Metro-North Railroad
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North Railroad is comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

payload.description: Metro-North Railroad is comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North Railroad is comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

payload.description: North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access Project
tool_args.raw_text: On December 9, residents of transit-starved areas of the East and South Bronx got an early holiday present as we broke ground on the Penn Station Access project. It'll involve the construction of four new, ADA-compliant Metro-North stations on Amtrak's Hell Gate Line which, upon completion, will serve as an extension of the New Haven Line from New Rochelle to Penn Station.

payload.description: Construction of four new ADA-compliant Metro-North stations on Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, serving as an extension of the New Haven Line from New Rochelle to Penn Station, with stations in Hunts Point, Parkchester/Van Nest, Morris Park, and Co-Op City.

evidence_refs.source_quote: On December 9...we broke ground on the Penn Station Access project. It'll involve the construction of four new, ADA-compliant Metro-North stations on Amtrak's Hell Gate Line...
Parkchester is one of the four new commuter rail stations slated to be constructed in the Bronx as part of the MTA's Penn Station Access Project.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block
warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Grand Central Madison
tool_args.raw_text: The new 700,000 square-foot LIRR terminal nearing completion below Grand Central Terminal shall herein and henceforth be known as Grand Central Madison

payload.description: New 700,000 square-foot LIRR terminal below Grand Central Terminal providing direct LIRR service to the East Side of Manhattan.

evidence_refs.source_quote: the new 700,000 square-foot LIRR terminal nearing completion below Grand Central Terminal shall herein and henceforth be known as Grand Central Madison
MTA Construction and Development turned operational control over to LIRR

tool_args.label: Main Line Third Track
tool_args.raw_text: the third and final segment of the long-awaited, and now fully operational, third track between Floral Park and Hicksville

payload.description: Third and final segment completed and fully operational - a 9.8-mile span giving LIRR more operational flexibility, improving safety, minimizing disruptions, increasing systemwide service by 41% with implementation of full service to Grand Central.

evidence_refs.source_quote: the third and final segment of the long-awaited, and now fully operational, third track between Floral Park and Hicksville
new 9.8-mile span that gives the LIRR more operational flexibility to improve safety, minimize disruptions, increase systemwide service by 41%

tool_args.label: Park Avenue Viaduct Replacement Project
tool_args.raw_text: The Park Avenue Viaduct Replacement Project...will replace the elevated steel structure that carries four tracks along Park Avenue between East 110 Street and the Harlem River Lift Bridge.

payload.description: Replace the elevated steel structure that carries four tracks along Park Avenue between East 110 Street and the Harlem River Lift Bridge. Major construction expected to begin fall 2023, anticipated in service 2027.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Park Avenue Viaduct Replacement Project...will replace the elevated steel structure that carries four tracks along Park Avenue between East 110 Street and the Harlem River Lift Bridge.
In the fall of 2023, major construction is expected to begin, and the repaired viaduct is anticipated to be in service in 2027.

tool_args.label: Mount Vernon Bridge Replacement Program
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development's continues its work to replace all century-old overhead bridges across the Metro-North tracks in the City of Mount Vernon

payload.description: Replacement of the remaining two century-old overhead bridges (Fulton Avenue and South Street) across the New Haven Line in Mount Vernon. Program completed replacement of six other overhead bridges already.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Construction & Development's continues its work to replace all century-old overhead bridges across the Metro-North tracks in the City of Mount Vernon, and the final two bridges are slated to be replaced.
The remaining two bridges on Fulton Avenue and South Street...

tool_args.label: Wickers Creek Culvert Replacement
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development and Metro-North wrapped-up a six-month renewal project to replace the 110-year-old Wickers Creek Culvert in Dobbs Ferry, which was destroyed by Tropical Storm Ida

payload.description: Six-month project to replace the 110-year-old Wickers Creek Culvert destroyed by Tropical Storm Ida, including rebuilding the 4-track right of way above it. Completed March 2022. Resulted in faster travel times and increased capacity on the Hudson Line.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Construction & Development and Metro-North wrapped-up a six-month renewal project to replace the 110-year-old Wickers Creek Culvert in Dobbs Ferry, which was destroyed by Tropical Storm Ida the previous September.

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access Project

tool_args.label: Jamaica Capacity Improvements Project
tool_args.raw_text: As part of the Jamaica Capacity Improvements Project (JCI), LIRR is expanding Hall Interlocking near Jamaica Station.

payload.description: Multi-phase project to modernize critical LIRR infrastructure in and around Jamaica Station, expanding Hall Interlocking with new tracks, bridge construction, and Platform F. Project anticipated to be substantially completed by end of 2026.

tool_args.label: Park Avenue Viaduct Project
tool_args.raw_text: The program was created as part of the Park Avenue Viaduct Project, which will replace the elevated steel structure that carries four tracks along Park Avenue between East 110 Street and the Harlem River Lift Bridge.

payload.description: Replace the elevated steel structure that carries four tracks along Park Avenue between East 110 Street and the Harlem River Lift Bridge. Major construction expected to begin fall 2023, anticipated in service 2027.

tool_args.label: Mount Vernon Bridge Replacement Program
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development's (MTACD) continues its work to replace all century-old overhead bridges across the Metro-North tracks in the City of Mount Vernon, and the final two bridges are slated to be replaced.

payload.description: Replacement of the remaining two century-old overhead bridges (Fulton Avenue and South Street) across the New Haven Line in Mount Vernon. Program completed replacement of six other overhead bridges already.

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access Groundbreaking
tool_args.raw_text: On December 9...we broke ground on the Penn Station Access project.

payload.description: Groundbreaking ceremony for the Penn Station Access project, with four new Metro-North stations in the Bronx.

tool_args.label: LIRR 2021 OTP Record High

tool_args.label: Metro-North 2021 OTP Second Highest
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North announced a 97.1% total OTP for 2021—not quite the record, but it's only the second time it cracked the 97% mark for two consecutive years since the railroad's 1883 founding.

tool_args.raw_text: The Park Avenue Viaduct carries all of Metro-North's Grand Central Terminal trains, which comprise 98% of the service the railroad operates, but is a single point of failure.
